explicit_text: I was fortunate to win a National Defense Education Act fellowship to pursue my PhD, which I hadn't initially planned. This was during the time when Sputnik had just launched, and the government was eager to fund advanced degrees to catch up with Russia. The Act helped me immensely, even though I never repaid the government for their investment.

The war had a direct impact on the job market and my career prospects in the early 1970s.

explicit_text: In Madison County, Arkansas, I worked with colleagues to petition to outlaw phenoxy herbicides that were being sprayed to clear forests for cattle pasture. We researched voter registration records at the courthouse to gather signatures and put the issue on the ballot. Although we did not win, it was a significant community effort in environmental activism during the 1970s.

entity: University of Oklahoma Health Sciences Center
explicit_text: I received treatment for rheumatoid arthritis at the University of Oklahoma Health Sciences Center's rheumatoid arthritis clinic in Tulsa. They tried various therapies including gold salts injections before newer immune-suppressing medicines became available in the 1990s. The center was crucial in managing my chronic illness.

entity: There Is a Cure for Arthritis
explicit_text: I tried the advice in the book There Is a Cure for Arthritis during the 1970s when my symptoms were worsening. The book emphasized diet changes, like juice fasts and reducing flour and sugar, which helped me lose weight and feel better. It was an important part of managing my arthritis before modern medicines were available.
